description: A minimal blog with ability to view a list of posts and read individual posts. Backend stores posts in-memory, frontend displays them.

tasks.task: Create a blog module with in-memory storage. Create src/blog/blog.module.ts, src/blog/blog.controller.ts, and src/blog/blog.service.ts following the existing NestJS patterns (see app.controller.ts and app.service.ts for reference). The service should store posts in a simple array with id, title, content, and createdAt fields. Implement GET /posts (list all posts) and GET /posts/:id (get single post) endpoints. Seed 2-3 sample posts. Register BlogModule in app.module.ts imports array. Enable CORS in main.ts using app.enableCors() so the frontend can connect.

Build a simple blog UI. Create src/components/PostList.tsx to fetch and display posts from http://localhost:3000/posts. Create src/components/PostDetail.tsx to show a single post. Update src/App.tsx to manage state for which view to show (list vs detail) using useState - no router needed. Add a simple navigation by clicking on post titles to view details and a 'Back to posts' link. Add basic styles to src/style.css for .post-list, .post-item, .post-detail classes matching the existing dark theme.

example_backend: GET /posts returns array of posts with id, title, content, createdAt
CORS headers are present in responses
example_frontend: Blog post list loads and displays post titles
Clicking a post title shows the full post content
Back button returns to post list
